items.title: All entry-level jobs require 3-5 years of experience. How am I supposed to get that if no one will hire me?
items.description: How to get experience if no one is willing to be your first employer.

items.title: ‘I’d hate to end up with an unexpected tax bill’: I’m 73 and still work full time. Can I avoid paying taxes on my Social Security benefits?
items.description: “I’m actually earning more each week than I ever have before.”

items.title: Rocket Lab’s stock could surge 250% as the company takes a page out of SpaceX’s book, analyst says
items.description: Morgan Stanley says the bull case for the rocket-launch stock is now much rosier as the company diversifies its business.

items.title: Oil prices touch two-week highs  after Trump suggests U.S.-Iran cease-fire is over
items.description: Oil prices touched their highest levels in more than two weeks on Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he believed the cease-fire with Iran was over.

items.title: Why the stock market’s biggest laggards might be your best defense against a summer selloff
items.description: Low-volatility stocks are having a moment after a long stretch of underperformance.

items.title: Insurance giant says it’s turning to AI to replace as many as 1,800 jobs
items.description: Allianz Partners will axe between 1,500 and 1,800 roles from its workforce of close to 23,000

items.title: ‘I’ll happily wait’: Does delaying Social Security make sense for high earners like me?
items.description: “In many states, Social Security benefits are exempt from state income tax.”

items.title: ‘Our family is broken beyond repair’: My brother took over my parents’ finances. What can I do?
items.description: “Without telling me, my brother petitioned for sole guardianship of our mother.”

items.title: I started retirement with $3 million. Now I’m 89 and down to $2 million. What if I get sick?
items.description: Even if you’re not worried about your normal spending anymore, a long-term-care need is still a problem for any budget.

items.title: As a red-hot global stock market stumbles into bear territory, this Wall Street bull spots a dip worth buying
items.description: Tom Lee of Fundstrat says he’s buying the dip, and not scared off by Samsung’s losses
